top_anchors.claim: PJM 2027/28 capacity cleared at FERC cap

consensus_variance.narrative: Street's 2027-2028 EBITDA implicitly marks the ~35% open 2028 generation book at/above EIA STEO base and extrapolates cap-level capacity revenue. EIA modeling shows PJM upside ~+4% even in a high-demand scenario and ERCOT upside is high-demand-scenario-only; the cap/collar expires after 2029/30. Our variance shifts ~10pp of probability from base/bull into the bear tails (open book reprices -15-20%, capacity reverts toward floor), implying ~-$0.6 to -$0.9B vs a peak-extrapolated 2028. Two-tailed (ERCOT data-center load is a genuine upside), hence Hold not Sell.

evidence_refs.ref: VST hedge ratios 98/89/65% for 2026/27/28 (10-Q / IR) -> ~35% of 2028 open
EIA STEO power-price scenarios: PJM high-demand upside ~+4%; ERCOT spike high-demand-only; no published 2028 price
PJM cap/collar settlement expires after 2029/30 (FERC filing)

revision_velocity.breadth_note: PT actions ~1.3-1.5:1 up vs down; estimate breadth positive; one upgrade (Jefferies Hold->Buy 2/10), zero downgrades 3mo.

revision_velocity.pre_print_drift: Beats fade (Q1'26 +EPS beat closed -2.74%); next earnings 2026-08-06; implied move ~+/-6-8%.
revision_velocity.peer_comparison: VST revisions neutral-to-better than CEG/NRG/TLN (FY26 EPS revised up); sector-wide multiple de-rate (CEG -14% YTD with estimates held).
revision_velocity.verdict: De-rate (multiple compression, estimates intact-to-rising), NOT deterioration.
